title: Analysis
text:
Analysis of Forest Fires in Turkey
This page presents the exploratory data analysis of forest fire statistics in Turkey between 1988 and 2024.


Key Takeaways

Forest fires in Turkey fluctuate significantly over time.
Certain years experienced extremely high burned forest areas.
Burned areas and fire frequency do not always increase together.
Forest fires remain an important environmental issue in Turkey.



Load Libraries


Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors


Code
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)



Attaching package: 'janitor'

The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    chisq.test, fisher.test




Import Dataset


Code
fires <- read_excel("forest_fires.xlsx") %>%
  clean_names()

names(fires)


[1] "year"            "burned_area"     "number_of_fires"




Rename Columns


Code
fires <- fires %>%
  rename(
    year = 1,
    burned_area = 2,
    number_of_fires = 3
  )

glimpse(fires)


Rows: 37
Columns: 3
$ year            <dbl> 1988, 1989, 1990, 1991, 1992, 1993, 1994, 1995, 1996, …
$ burned_area     <dbl> 18210.0, 13099.0, 13742.0, 8081.0, 12232.0, 15393.0, 3…
$ number_of_fires <dbl> 1372, 1633, 1750, 1481, 2117, 2545, 3239, 1770, 1645, …


Code
summary(fires)


      year       burned_area     number_of_fires
 Min.   :1988   Min.   :  2821   Min.   :1339   
 1st Qu.:1997   1st Qu.:  6317   1st Qu.:1770   
 Median :2006   Median : 10454   Median :2150   
 Mean   :2006   Mean   : 14949   Mean   :2252   
 3rd Qu.:2015   3rd Qu.: 14922   3rd Qu.:2579   
 Max.   :2024   Max.   :139503   Max.   :3797   




Number of Forest Fires by Year


Code
ggplot(fires, aes(x = year, y = number_of_fires)) +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  geom_point(color = "darkred") +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Forest Fires by Year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Fires"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









The graph shows yearly fluctuations in the number of forest fires in Turkey.


Burned Forest Area by Year


Code
ggplot(fires, aes(x = year, y = burned_area)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_point(color = "forestgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Forest Area by Year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Burned Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









The burned forest area changes significantly across years.


Relationship Between Fire Numbers and Burned Area


Code
ggplot(fires, aes(x = number_of_fires, y = burned_area)) +
  geom_point(color = "orange", size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Number of Fires and Burned Area",
    x = "Number of Fires",
    y = "Burned Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









Some years experienced fewer fires but larger burned forest areas.


Conclusion
Forest fires continue to be an important environmental issue in Turkey. The analysis shows that both fire frequency and burned area vary considerably over time.

title: Data
text:
Data


Data Source
The dataset used in this project was obtained from the official statistics published by the General Directorate of Forestry (OGM), the institution responsible for forest management and forest fire monitoring in Turkey.
The official data source can be accessed from the following link:
OGM Official Statistics


About the Dataset
This dataset contains forest fire statistics in Turkey between 1988 and 2024.
The dataset includes:

Number of forest fires by year
Total burned forest area
Fire statistics over time
Information related to forest fire frequency and severity

The data was selected because forest fires have become an important environmental and socio-economic issue in Turkey in recent years.


Importing the Dataset


Code
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
library(janitor)



Attaching package: 'janitor'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    chisq.test, fisher.test


Code
fires <- read_excel("forest_fires.xlsx") %>%
  clean_names()

head(fires)


# A tibble: 6 × 3
   year burned_area number_of_fires
  <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>
1  1988       18210            1372
2  1989       13099            1633
3  1990       13742            1750
4  1991        8081            1481
5  1992       12232            2117
6  1993       15393            2545




Data Preparation
The dataset was cleaned and prepared for analysis.
The following preprocessing steps were applied:

Column names were standardized
Data structure was checked
Variables were prepared for visualization and analysis



Code
glimpse(fires)


Rows: 37
Columns: 3
$ year            <dbl> 1988, 1989, 1990, 1991, 1992, 1993, 1994, 1995, 1996, …
$ burned_area     <dbl> 18210.0, 13099.0, 13742.0, 8081.0, 12232.0, 15393.0, 3…
$ number_of_fires <dbl> 1372, 1633, 1750, 1481, 2117, 2545, 3239, 1770, 1645, …


Code
summary(fires)


      year       burned_area     number_of_fires
 Min.   :1988   Min.   :  2821   Min.   :1339   
 1st Qu.:1997   1st Qu.:  6317   1st Qu.:1770   
 Median :2006   Median : 10454   Median :2150   
 Mean   :2006   Mean   : 14949   Mean   :2252   
 3rd Qu.:2015   3rd Qu.: 14922   3rd Qu.:2579   
 Max.   :2024   Max.   :139503   Max.   :3797   




Final Dataset
The cleaned dataset was saved in .RData format for reproducibility.


Code
save(fires, file = "final_forest_fires.RData")


The final dataset can be downloaded below:
Download Final Dataset
